title: draba canoensis
intro: Draba canoensis is a species of flowering plant in the family Brassicaceae, commonly known as the yellow draba or yellow whitlow grass. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The plant is valued for its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which are believed to contribute to its health benefits, including the reduction of pain and the promotion of wound healing. Draba canoensis is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and may be used in the form of a tea, tincture, or salve, depending on the desired application and cultural tradition.
